<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 320 130" width="320" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="160" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Metal vs Plastic Spoon in Hot Drink</text>
  <rect x="15" y="35" width="135" height="75" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="83" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#dc2626">Metal spoon ★</text>
  <text x="83" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Good conductor</text>
  <text x="83" y="86" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Gets HOTTER</text>
  <text x="83" y="100" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">(conducts heat fast)</text>
  <rect x="170" y="35" width="135" height="75" fill="#f8fafc" stroke="#e2e8f0" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="238" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#475569">Plastic spoon</text>
  <text x="238" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">Poor conductor</text>
  <text x="238" y="86" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">Stays cooler</text>
  <text x="238" y="100" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">(insulator)</text>
  <text x="160" y="120" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Metal spoon hotter than plastic = better heat conductor ★</text>
</svg>
options: Metal conducts heat better to the hand
correct_answer: Metal conducts heat better to the hand

template: Wearing light-colored clothes in hot weather helps because:
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 340 130" width="340" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="170" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Light Colors vs Dark Colors — Heat</text>
  <rect x="15" y="35" width="145" height="75" fill="#f8fafc" stroke="#e2e8f0" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="88" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#475569">Light colors ★</text>
  <text x="88" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">Reflect more heat</text>
  <text x="88" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">Absorb less</text>
  <text x="88" y="98" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">Stays cooler ★</text>
  <rect x="180" y="35" width="145" height="75" fill="#334155" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="253" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="white">Dark colors</text>
  <text x="253" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#fde68a">Absorb more heat</text>
  <text x="253" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#fde68a">Reflect less</text>
  <text x="253" y="98" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#fde68a">Gets hotter</text>
  <text x="170" y="120" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Light colors reflect more heat radiation, stay cooler ★</text>
</svg>

template: A double-walled vacuum flask keeps liquids hot/cold because:
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 320 130" width="320" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="160" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Vacuum Flask — Keeps Drink Hot</text>
  <rect x="105" y="30" width="110" height="85" fill="#f8fafc" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="2" rx="6"/>
  <rect x="117" y="42" width="86" height="65" fill="#334155" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <rect x="119" y="44" width="82" height="61" fill="#bae6fd" rx="3"/>
  <text x="160" y="75" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">HOT liquid</text>
  <text x="115" y="85" font-size="7" fill="#dc2626">vacuum</text>
  <text x="115" y="96" font-size="7" fill="#dc2626">wall ★</text>
  <defs><marker id="arrVF" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#dc2626"/></marker></defs>
  <line x1="110" y1="70" x2="95" y2="55" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arrVF)"/>
  <text x="50" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">blocks</text>
  <text x="50" y="66" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">conduction</text>
  <text x="50" y="77" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">convection ★</text>
  <text x="160" y="125" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Vacuum flask: vacuum prevents conduction + convection ★</text>
</svg>
options: Vacuum allows heat to pass through
Vacuum prevents heat transfer by conduction and convection
correct_answer: Vacuum prevents heat transfer by conduction and convection

template: Light travels in:
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Light Travels in Straight Lines</text>
  <circle cx="45" cy="72" r="22" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="45" y="76" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">☀️</text>
  <line x1="67" y1="72" x2="250" y2="72" stroke="#f97316" stroke-width="3"/>
  <line x1="67" y1="60" x2="250" y2="60" stroke="#f97316" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="3,3"/>
  <line x1="67" y1="84" x2="250" y2="84" stroke="#f97316" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="3,3"/>
  <text x="160" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#f97316">light rays →</text>
  <rect x="250" y="55" width="10" height="35" fill="#334155" rx="2"/>
  <text x="275" y="75" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">screen</text>
  <text x="150" y="106" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Light travels in STRAIGHT LINES (rectilinear propagation) ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="120" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">This is why shadows are sharp-edged</text>
</svg>

template: An opaque object is one that:
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 360 130" width="360" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="180" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Opacity of Materials</text>
  <rect x="10" y="35" width="80" height="70" fill="#bae6fd" stroke="#0ea5e9" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/>
  <text x="50" y="56" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">Clear glass</text>
  <text x="50" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">Transparent</text>
  <text x="50" y="83" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#0369a1">all light ✓</text>
  <rect x="98" y="35" width="80" height="70" fill="#e2e8f0" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="138" y="56" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">Frosted glass</text>
  <text x="138" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">Translucent</text>
  <text x="138" y="83" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">some light</text>
  <rect x="186" y="28" width="100" height="84" fill="#334155" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="236" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="white">Opaque ★</text>
  <text x="236" y="67" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#fde68a">NO light passes</text>
  <text x="236" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#fde68a">Casts shadow</text>
  <text x="236" y="95" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#94a3b8">wood, metal, stone</text>
  <rect x="294" y="35" width="62" height="70" fill="#334155" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="325" y="58" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="white">Metal</text>
  <text x="325" y="74" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#94a3b8">Opaque</text>
  <text x="180" y="120" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Opaque = no light passes through → casts shadow ★</text>
</svg>
options: Allows all light to pass through
Allows some light to pass through
Does not allow light to pass through
correct_answer: Does not allow light to pass through

template: When light bounces off a surface, it is called:
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 280 135" width="280" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="140" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Light Reflection off Surface</text>
  <defs><marker id="arrRL" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#f97316"/></marker></defs>
  <rect x="20" y="100" width="240" height="12" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="140" y="122" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">mirror / surface</text>
  <line x1="45" y1="35" x2="140" y2="100" stroke="#f97316" stroke-width="2.5" marker-end="url(#arrRL)"/>
  <text x="60" y="60" font-size="9" fill="#f97316">incident ray</text>
  <line x1="140" y1="100" x2="235" y2="35" stroke="#f97316" stroke-width="2.5" marker-end="url(#arrRL)"/>
  <text x="200" y="60" font-size="9" fill="#f97316">reflected ray</text>
  <line x1="140" y1="30" x2="140" y2="100" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4,3"/>
  <text x="148" y="58" font-size="8" fill="#94a3b8">normal</text>
  <rect x="80" y="102" width="120" height="18" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="3"/>
  <text x="140" y="115" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">REFLECTION ★</text>
  <text x="140" y="130" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Light bouncing off surface = reflection ★</text>
</svg>

  <line x1="135" y1="73" x2="200" y2="75" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrPR)"/>
  <line x1="135" y1="78" x2="200" y2="88" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrPR)"/>
  <line x1="135" y1="82" x2="200" y2="100" stroke="#7c3aed" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrPR)"/>
  <text x="215" y="42" font-size="7" fill="#dc2626">Red</text>
  <text x="215" y="100" font-size="7" fill="#7c3aed">Violet</text>

</svg>

template: We can see objects because:

  <rect x="119" y="40" width="60" height="35" fill="#f8fafc" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/>

  <line x1="179" y1="58" x2="222" y2="66" stroke="#f97316" stroke-width="2.5" marker-end="url(#arrSEE)"/>

</svg>
options: Light from sources reflects off objects into our eyes

template: Sound cannot travel through:
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 340 130" width="340" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="170" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Sound Cannot Travel Through Vacuum</text>
  <rect x="10" y="35" width="80" height="70" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/>
  <text x="50" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Air</text>
  <text x="50" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">✓ sound travels</text>
  <rect x="98" y="35" width="80" height="70" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/>
  <text x="138" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Water</text>
  <text x="138" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">✓ sound travels</text>
  <rect x="186" y="35" width="80" height="70" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/>
  <text x="226" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Steel</text>
  <text x="226" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">✓ sound travels</text>
  <rect x="274" y="28" width="62" height="84" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="305" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="#dc2626">Vacuum ★</text>
  <text x="305" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">no particles</text>
  <text x="305" y="83" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">✗ no sound</text>
  <text x="170" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Sound needs particles to travel — cannot travel through vacuum ★</text>
</svg>

template: A high-pitched sound has:
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">High Pitch = High Frequency</text>
  <rect x="10" y="35" width="130" height="75" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="75" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">High pitch ★</text>
  <path d="M20,88 Q30,60 40,88 Q50,60 60,88 Q70,60 80,88 Q90,60 100,88 Q110,60 120,88" fill="none" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="75" y="105" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">High frequency vibrations ★</text>
  <rect x="158" y="35" width="130" height="75" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="223" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Low pitch</text>
  <path d="M165,88 Q185,60 205,88 Q225,60 245,88 Q265,60 280,88" fill="none" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="223" y="105" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Low frequency vibrations</text>
  <text x="150" y="122" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">Pitch ∝ frequency (more waves per second = higher pitch) ★</text>
</svg>

template: Volume (loudness) of sound depends on:
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Volume (Loudness) = Amplitude</text>
  <rect x="10" y="35" width="130" height="75" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="75" y="53" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Loud ★</text>
  <path d="M18,72 Q35,40 52,72 Q69,104 86,72 Q103,40 120,72" fill="none" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="3"/>
  <text x="75" y="103" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Large amplitude ★</text>

template: For electricity to flow, a circuit must be:
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 320 130" width="320" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">

  <rect x="15" y="35" width="130" height="75" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="80" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Closed circuit ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Complete loop ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="84" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">💡 Bulb lights up</text>
  <text x="80" y="98" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">electricity flows</text>
  <rect x="175" y="35" width="130" height="75" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="240" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Open circuit</text>
  <text x="240" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Broken loop</text>
  <text x="240" y="84" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">💡 Bulb stays dark</text>

  <text x="160" y="120" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Electricity only flows in a CLOSED (complete) circuit ★</text>
</svg>

Allow some light but objects can't be seen clearly

template: {CHARACTER_0} shines light at a mirror. The angle of reflection equals:

  <defs><marker id="arrMR" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#64748b"/></marker></defs>

  <text x="130" y="42" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#f97316">incident ray</text>
  <line x1="200" y1="70" x2="100" y2="110" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2.5" marker-end="url(#arrMR)"/>
  <text x="130" y="102" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#3b82f6">reflected ray</text>
  <line x1="200" y1="25" x2="200" y2="115" stroke="#334155" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4,3"/>

</svg>

template: Question: What is the independent variable (what {CHARACTER_0} changes) in this experiment?

  <rect x="15" y="30" width="270" height="80" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="150" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Independent variable ★</text>

  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Independent variable = the factor deliberately changed ★</text>
</svg>

template: Question: Why does the bulb stay lit in Circuit B?
experiment_setup:
{CHARACTER_0} sets up two circuits with 2 bulbs each:
• Circuit A: Bulbs connected in series
• Circuit B: Bulbs connected in parallel
experiment_data: 📊 Observation: In Circuit A, when one bulb is removed, the other bulb goes out. In Circuit B, when one bulb is removed, the other bulb stays lit.
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 340 130" width="340" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="170" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Circuit B — Bulb Stays Lit (Parallel)</text>
  <rect x="10" y="30" width="155" height="80" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2" rx="6"/>
  <text x="88" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="#dc2626">Circuit A (Series)</text>
  <text x="88" y="65" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">One bulb off → ALL off ✗</text>
  <text x="88" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">One path only ✗</text>
  <rect x="178" y="30" width="155" height="80" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="256" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Circuit B (Parallel) ★</text>
  <text x="256" y="65" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Own separate path ★</text>
  <text x="256" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Bulb stays lit ★</text>
  <text x="170" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Parallel circuit: each bulb has own path → stays lit ★</text>
</svg>
options: Parallel circuits use more powerful batteries
In parallel circuits, each bulb has its own path to the battery
The remaining bulb produces its own electricity
correct_answer: In parallel circuits, each bulb has its own path to the battery

template: Question: What conclusion can {CHARACTER_0} make about shadow length?
experiment_setup: {CHARACTER_0} places an object between a light source and a screen. {CHARACTER_0} moves the light source to different heights while keeping the object in the same position.

  <rect x="50" y="100" width="80" height="10" fill="#e2e8f0"/>

  <rect x="200" y="35" width="90" height="80" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="245" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Conclusion ★</text>

Shadow length depends on the object's color

template: Question: What does this experiment prove about sound?
experiment_setup: {CHARACTER_0} places a ringing phone in a bell jar and gradually pumps out the air while listening to the sound.
experiment_data:
📊 Observations:
• Full air: Loud ringing heard clearly
• Half air removed: Softer ringing
• Most air removed: Very faint sound
• Complete vacuum: No sound heard
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Sound Needs a Medium to Travel</text>
  <rect x="15" y="30" width="270" height="80" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="150" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Sound needs medium ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Cannot travel through vacuum ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Travels through solid/liquid/gas ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">(No particles = no sound)</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Experiment proves: sound cannot travel without a medium ★</text>
</svg>
options: Sound can travel through a vacuum
Sound needs a medium (like air) to travel
Sound travels better in a vacuum
correct_answer: Sound needs a medium (like air) to travel

template: Question: What can {CHARACTER_0} conclude?
experiment_setup:
{CHARACTER_0} sets up 4 plant seedlings in identical conditions except for one variable:
• Plant A: Sunlight + water
• Plant B: No sunlight + water
• Plant C: Sunlight + no water
• Plant D: No sunlight + no water

diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Conclusion — More Light, More Growth</text>
  <rect x="15" y="30" width="270" height="80" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="150" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Conclusion ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">↑ Light intensity</text>
  <text x="150" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">→ ↑ plant growth ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">(more photosynthesis)</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">More light = more photosynthesis = more growth ★</text>
</svg>
options: Plants only need water to survive
Plants only need sunlight to survive
Plants need BOTH sunlight and water for healthy growth
Plants can survive without sunlight or water
correct_answer: Plants need BOTH sunlight and water for healthy growth

  <text x="150" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Acid rain damages leaves ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Less photosynthesis ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Pollution (acid rain) damages leaves → reduces plant growth ★</text>
</svg>
options: Pollution harms plant growth and health
Plants are not affected by water quality
correct_answer: Pollution harms plant growth and health

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">

template: Question: Which sense is MOST important for identifying food?
experiment_setup: {CHARACTER_0} conducts a test where a blindfolded volunteer tries to identify different foods while holding their nose closed, then with nose open.
experiment_data:
📊 Results:
With nose held closed:
• Apple vs Potato: Could not tell the difference (identified 2/10 correctly)
• Lemon vs Orange juice: Could only tell sour vs sweet (5/10 correct)

With nose open:
• Identified correctly: 9/10 foods
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Identifying Food — Taste Most Important</text>
  <rect x="15" y="30" width="270" height="80" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="150" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Most important sense ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">👅 Taste ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">(combined with smell)</text>
  <text x="150" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">Helps identify type of food ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Taste (and smell) = most important for identifying food ★</text>
</svg>
options: Touch is most important for tasting

Vision is the only sense needed

template: Question: What does this demonstrate about digestion?
experiment_setup:
{CHARACTER_0} models the action of saliva in digestion:
• Test tube A: Bread + water
• Test tube B: Bread + saliva

Both kept at body temperature (37°C) for 10 minutes, then iodine solution added to test for starch.
experiment_data:
📊 Observations:
• Test tube A: Iodine turns blue-black (starch present)
• Test tube B: Iodine stays brown (no starch - it was broken down)

Taste test before iodine: Test tube B liquid tastes slightly sweet.
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Digestion — Breaks Down Large Molecules</text>
  <defs><marker id="arrDIG" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#64748b"/></marker></defs>
  <rect x="20" y="40" width="90" height="55" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/>
  <text x="65" y="65" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">Large food</text>
  <text x="65" y="79" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#92400e">molecules</text>
  <line x1="110" y1="67" x2="142" y2="67" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2.5" marker-end="url(#arrDIG)"/>
  <text x="126" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">enzymes</text>
  <rect x="143" y="33" width="140" height="72" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="213" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Small molecules ★</text>
  <text x="213" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Absorbed into blood ★</text>
  <text x="213" y="86" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">for body use</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Digestion breaks large food molecules into small absorbable ones ★</text>
</svg>
options: Saliva has no effect on food
Saliva contains enzymes that break down starch into sugars
Water breaks down starch better than saliva
correct_answer: Saliva contains enzymes that break down starch into sugars

template: Question: What conditions are needed for mold growth?
experiment_setup:
{CHARACTER_0} places pieces of bread in different conditions to observe mold growth:

• Bread B: In open air, dry, warm place

• Bread D: In refrigerator, sealed bag, moist
experiment_data:

• Bread A: Heavy mold growth (green and black)
• Bread B: Slight mold, mostly dried out
• Bread C: Very little mold
• Bread D: Minimal mold

  <text x="80" y="83" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">💧 Moisture ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="98" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Both needed ★</text>
  <rect x="160" y="35" width="130" height="70" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="225" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Mold inhibited:</text>
  <text x="225" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Cold fridge ★</text>
  <text x="225" y="81" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Dry conditions ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Mold needs warmth AND moisture to grow ★</text>
</svg>
options: Mold grows best in cold, dry conditions
Mold grows best in warm, moist conditions
Mold needs dry conditions to grow

template: Question: What is the role of pollen in reproduction?
experiment_setup:
{CHARACTER_0} sets up an experiment with identical flowering plants:
• Plant Group A: Pollen allowed to reach stigma naturally
• Plant Group B: Stigma covered with bag to prevent pollen from reaching it
• Plant Group C: Pollen manually transferred to stigma with brush
experiment_data:
📊 After 4 weeks:
• Plant A: Developed seeds and fruits
• Plant B: No seeds or fruits developed, flower died
• Plant C: Developed seeds and fruits (similar to A)
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Pollen — Fertilizes Egg for Reproduction</text>
  <rect x="15" y="30" width="270" height="80" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="150" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Role of pollen ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Carries male gametes ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Fertilizes egg → seed forms ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">Transferred by insects/wind</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Pollen fertilizes egg cell → new plant (seed) formed ★</text>
</svg>
options: Pollen is not needed for plant reproduction
Pollen must reach the stigma for fertilization and seed formation
Only Group A can make seeds
correct_answer: Pollen must reach the stigma for fertilization and seed formation

template: Question: What conclusion can {CHARACTER_0} make about the life cycle?
experiment_setup: {CHARACTER_0} observes the complete life cycle of mealworms (beetle larvae) over several weeks, documenting each stage with photos and measurements.
experiment_data:
📊 Life cycle observed:
• Week 0-2: Tiny eggs → larvae hatched
• Week 2-6: Larvae grew larger, molted several times
• Week 6-8: Larvae → pupae (non-moving stage)
• Week 8-10: Pupae → adult beetles emerged
• Week 10+: Adult beetles laid new eggs

  <text x="150" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">each stage has different features</text>

</svg>

template: What is the difference between an observation and an inference?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 340 130" width="340" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="170" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Observation vs Inference</text>
  <rect x="10" y="28" width="155" height="84" fill="#bae6fd" stroke="#0ea5e9" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="88" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#0369a1">Observation ★</text>
  <text x="88" y="66" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">Uses 5 senses ★</text>
  <text x="88" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">e.g. "It is red"</text>
  <text x="88" y="94" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">"It feels rough"</text>
  <rect x="178" y="28" width="155" height="84" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="256" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Inference ★</text>
  <text x="256" y="66" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Based on observation</text>
  <text x="256" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">+ prior knowledge ★</text>
  <text x="256" y="94" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">e.g. "It is a tomato"</text>
  <text x="170" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Observation = senses; Inference = interpretation/conclusion ★</text>
</svg>
options: An observation is a guess; an inference is measured
An observation uses the senses to gather information; an inference is a conclusion based on observations
An observation requires equipment; an inference does not
There is no difference between them
correct_answer: An observation uses the senses to gather information; an inference is a conclusion based on observations

template: {CHARACTER_0} conducted an experiment to test how type of soil affects plant growth. He noticed that one plant in sandy soil grew much better than the others. Which statement best explains why this result may NOT be reliable?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Experiment Limitation — One Trial</text>
  <rect x="15" y="28" width="270" height="84" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="150" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#dc2626">Limitation ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Experiment only done ONCE ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Results may not be reliable</text>
  <text x="150" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Should repeat for accuracy ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Limitation: only done ONCE → results may not be reliable ★</text>
</svg>

The experiment was only done once, so it may not be repeatable
Plants always grow well in sandy soil
The result shows that all soils are the same
correct_answer: The experiment was only done once, so it may not be repeatable

template: A scientist measures the height of 5 bean plants grown in different types of fertilizer: 12 cm, 15 cm, 9 cm, 18 cm, and 11 cm. What is the average height of the bean plants?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 340 130" width="340" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="170" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Finding the Mean (Average)</text>
  <rect x="10" y="30" width="240" height="75" fill="#bae6fd" stroke="#0ea5e9" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/>
  <text x="50" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">Plant 1: 11cm</text>
  <text x="50" y="66" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">Plant 2: 14cm</text>
  <text x="50" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">Plant 3: 12cm</text>
  <text x="50" y="94" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">Plant 4: 15cm</text>
  <text x="155" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">Plant 5: 13cm</text>
  <text x="215" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">Sum:</text>
  <text x="215" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">65 ÷ 5 =</text>
  <rect x="267" y="30" width="65" height="75" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="300" y="65" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">13cm ★</text>
  <text x="170" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Mean = (11+14+12+15+13) ÷ 5 = 13cm ★</text>
</svg>

template: {CHARACTER_0} wants to find a fair way to test which running shoe provides the most grip. Which method best ensures a fair test?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Fair Shoe Test — Same Surface</text>
  <rect x="15" y="28" width="270" height="84" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="150" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Fair test ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Test ALL shoes on the SAME</text>
  <text x="150" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">surface, same person ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">Only shoe type changes</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Fair test: keep everything same except the shoe type ★</text>
</svg>
options: Test each shoe on different types of surfaces
Test all shoes on the same surface, with the same person, applying the same force
Have different people test each shoe
Test the shoes at different times of the day
correct_answer: Test all shoes on the same surface, with the same person, applying the same force

template: When recording results in an experiment, why is it important to repeat the experiment multiple times?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Repeat Experiment — More Reliable</text>
  <rect x="15" y="28" width="270" height="84" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="150" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Why repeat? ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">↑ Reliability ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">↑ Accuracy of results ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">Reduces error and anomalies</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Repeating increases reliability and reduces errors ★</text>
</svg>
options: To increase reliability and reduce the effect of errors
Because one result is not enough to fill in a table
correct_answer: To increase reliability and reduce the effect of errors

template: {CHARACTER_0} notices dark clouds forming before it rains. She predicts it will rain within an hour. What type of scientific skill is she using?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 340 130" width="340" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="170" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Dark Clouds → Rain — Predicting</text>
  <rect x="10" y="28" width="155" height="84" fill="#bae6fd" stroke="#0ea5e9" stroke-width="2" rx="6"/>
  <text x="88" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="#0369a1">Observation</text>
  <text x="88" y="65" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">☁️ Dark clouds</text>
  <text x="88" y="78" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">forming</text>
  <rect x="178" y="28" width="155" height="84" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="256" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Predicting ★</text>
  <text x="256" y="65" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">🌧️ It will rain</text>
  <text x="256" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Based on observation</text>
  <text x="256" y="94" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">+ knowledge ★</text>
  <text x="170" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Using observation to forecast = PREDICTING ★</text>
</svg>

template: In a well-designed experiment, why must only ONE variable be changed at a time?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Change ONE Variable at a Time</text>
  <rect x="15" y="28" width="270" height="84" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="150" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Fair test rule ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Only ONE variable changes ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">So we know what CAUSED</text>
  <text x="150" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">the difference ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">One variable → can identify which factor caused the result ★</text>
</svg>
options: Because it is too difficult to change more than one variable
To ensure we can identify which variable caused the change in results
Because experiments should be as short as possible
correct_answer: To ensure we can identify which variable caused the change in results

template: {CHARACTER_0} and {CHARACTER_1} performed the same experiment but got different results. What should they do to resolve this?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Different Results — Repeat the Experiment</text>
  <rect x="15" y="28" width="270" height="84" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="150" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">What to do ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Repeat experiment ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Find out which result is</text>
  <text x="150" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">more reliable ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Different results → repeat to determine more reliable result ★</text>
</svg>
options: Both should accept the average of their results without repeating
They should repeat the experiment carefully, checking all variables are controlled
They should choose the result that matches the hypothesis
correct_answer: They should repeat the experiment carefully, checking all variables are controlled

template: A student draws a bar graph to show the results of an experiment. What is the purpose of the graph?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Bar Graph — Display Data Visually</text>
  <rect x="30" y="40" width="15" height="60" fill="#22c55e" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <rect x="60" y="55" width="15" height="45" fill="#22c55e" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <rect x="90" y="50" width="15" height="50" fill="#22c55e" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <rect x="120" y="30" width="15" height="70" fill="#22c55e" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <line x1="25" y1="100" x2="165" y2="100" stroke="#334155" stroke-width="2"/>
  <line x1="25" y1="30" x2="25" y2="100" stroke="#334155" stroke-width="2"/>
  <rect x="175" y="33" width="120" height="72" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="235" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Purpose ★</text>
  <text x="235" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Display data clearly ★</text>
  <text x="235" y="83" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Easy to compare</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Bar graph displays data visually for easy comparison ★</text>
</svg>
options: To make the report look colorful
To display data in a clear, visual way that makes patterns easier to see
Graphs are more accurate than tables
correct_answer: To display data in a clear, visual way that makes patterns easier to see

template: {CHARACTER_0} observes that ice in a red cup melts faster than ice in a white cup left in the sun. He concludes: 'Dark-colored objects absorb more heat than light-colored objects.' Is this conclusion valid based on the experiment?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Valid Conclusion — Only One Variable Different</text>
  <rect x="15" y="28" width="270" height="84" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="150" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Valid because ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="65" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Only difference = cup COLOR ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Everything else same →</text>
  <text x="150" y="95" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">color caused the difference ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Valid conclusion: only cup color differs → color = cause ★</text>
</svg>
options: No, because ice always melts at the same rate regardless of cup color
Yes, because the only difference was cup color, supporting the conclusion
No, because only one trial was performed, so more repeats are needed
Yes, because the conclusion matches what the teacher taught
correct_answer: Yes, because the only difference was cup color, supporting the conclusion

template: Which of the following is an example of quantitative data (data that involves numbers)?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 340 130" width="340" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="170" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Quantitative Data — Numbers/Measurements</text>
  <rect x="10" y="28" width="155" height="84" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="88" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Quantitative ★</text>
  <text x="88" y="66" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Numbers/measurements ★</text>
  <text x="88" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Plant grew 5 cm ★</text>
  <text x="88" y="94" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Temperature: 30°C</text>
  <rect x="178" y="28" width="155" height="84" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="256" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Qualitative</text>
  <text x="256" y="64" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Descriptions ✗</text>
  <text x="256" y="78" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">"red flower"</text>
  <text x="256" y="92" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">"soil feels damp"</text>
  <text x="170" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Quantitative = involves numbers and measurements ★</text>
</svg>
options: The plant grew 5 cm in one week
correct_answer: The plant grew 5 cm in one week

template: What is the main cause of the enhanced greenhouse effect that is leading to global warming?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Enhanced Greenhouse Effect — Cause</text>
  <rect x="15" y="28" width="270" height="84" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="150" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#dc2626">Main cause ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="66" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">↑ Greenhouse gases ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">CO₂, CH₄, N₂O</text>
  <text x="150" y="94" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">from burning fossil fuels ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Enhanced greenhouse effect = increased greenhouse gas levels ★</text>
</svg>
options: The hole in the ozone layer
Increased levels of greenhouse gases from human activities
Changes in the Earth's orbit around the Sun
correct_answer: Increased levels of greenhouse gases from human activities

template: Carbon dioxide is called a greenhouse gas. What does it do in the atmosphere?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">CO₂ — Traps Heat in Atmosphere</text>
  <defs><marker id="arrCO2" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#64748b"/></marker></defs>
  <ellipse cx="150" cy="98" rx="100" ry="22" fill="#bae6fd" stroke="#0ea5e9" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="150" y="103" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">🌍 Earth</text>
  <ellipse cx="150" cy="55" rx="135" ry="28" fill="none" stroke="#f97316" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-dasharray="6,4"/>
  <text x="150" y="35" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#f97316">CO₂ layer</text>
  <text x="150" y="51" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#f97316">Traps heat ★ (doesn't escape)</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">CO₂ in atmosphere traps heat from Earth's surface ★</text>
</svg>
options: It makes the sky appear blue
It traps heat from the Earth's surface, preventing it from escaping into space
It filters harmful UV rays from the Sun
It produces oxygen for living things to breathe
correct_answer: It traps heat from the Earth's surface, preventing it from escaping into space

template: Which of the following human activities MOST contributes to the increase of greenhouse gases in the atmosphere?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Burning Fossil Fuels — Most CO₂</text>
  <rect x="15" y="28" width="130" height="84" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="80" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#dc2626">MOST CO₂ ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="66" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">⛽ Burning fossil</text>
  <text x="80" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">fuels ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="94" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">coal, oil, gas</text>
  <rect x="160" y="35" width="130" height="70" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="225" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Reduces CO₂:</text>
  <text x="225" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">🌳 Plant trees</text>
  <text x="225" y="84" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">☀️ Solar panels</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Burning fossil fuels = most contribution to greenhouse gases ★</text>
</svg>
options: Burning fossil fuels such as coal and oil for energy
correct_answer: Burning fossil fuels such as coal and oil for energy

template: Global warming causes sea levels to rise. What are the TWO main reasons for this?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Sea Level Rise — Melting Ice + Expansion</text>
  <rect x="15" y="28" width="130" height="84" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="80" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#dc2626">Two reasons ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="66" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">🧊 Polar ice melts ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">💧 Ocean expands ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="94" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">(thermal expansion)</text>
  <rect x="160" y="35" width="130" height="70" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="225" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Effects:</text>
  <text x="225" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Floods coastlines ★</text>
  <text x="225" y="84" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Loss of land</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Sea rise = melting ice caps + thermal expansion of water ★</text>
</svg>
options: Melting of polar ice caps and glaciers, AND thermal expansion of water as it warms
Evaporation of ocean water and condensation into clouds
Increased tidal activity and more frequent earthquakes
correct_answer: Melting of polar ice caps and glaciers, AND thermal expansion of water as it warms

template: Deforestation (cutting down forests) worsens climate change because:
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Deforestation Worsens Climate Change</text>
  <defs><marker id="arrDEF" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#64748b"/></marker></defs>
  <rect x="20" y="40" width="90" height="55" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/>
  <text x="65" y="62" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">🌳 Forests</text>
  <text x="65" y="76" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#15803d">absorb CO₂</text>
  <line x1="110" y1="67" x2="140" y2="67" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2.5" marker-end="url(#arrDEF)"/>
  <text x="125" y="58" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">cut ✗</text>
  <rect x="141" y="33" width="145" height="72" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="214" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="#dc2626">Less CO₂ absorbed ★</text>
  <text x="214" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">↑ CO₂ in atmosphere</text>
  <text x="214" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">↑ global warming ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Fewer trees = less CO₂ absorbed = more warming ★</text>
</svg>
options: Trees produce carbon dioxide which causes cooling
Fewer trees means less carbon dioxide is absorbed from the atmosphere
Trees release harmful gases that protect the Earth
Deforestation has no effect on the atmosphere
correct_answer: Fewer trees means less carbon dioxide is absorbed from the atmosphere

template: Which of the following actions can individuals do to reduce their carbon footprint (amount of greenhouse gases produced)?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Reduce Carbon Footprint Individually</text>
  <rect x="15" y="30" width="130" height="80" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="80" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Good actions ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">🚌 Public transport ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="83" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">💡 Save energy ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="98" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">♻️ Reuse + recycle</text>
  <rect x="160" y="35" width="130" height="70" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="225" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Bad actions:</text>
  <text x="225" y="65" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Drive alone ✗</text>
  <text x="225" y="78" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Leave AC on ✗</text>
  <text x="225" y="91" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Buy new always ✗</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Use public transport, save energy, recycle → lower carbon ★</text>
</svg>
options: Drive private cars everywhere and take frequent flights
Leave all lights and air conditioning on when not in the room
Use public transport, save energy at home, and reduce meat consumption
Buy new items frequently and throw away old ones
correct_answer: Use public transport, save energy at home, and reduce meat consumption

template: Biodiversity refers to the variety of life in an ecosystem. Why is high biodiversity important?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Biodiversity — More Stable Ecosystem</text>
  <rect x="15" y="30" width="130" height="80" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="80" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">High biodiversity ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">More species ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="83" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">More stable eco-</text>
  <text x="80" y="98" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">system ★</text>
  <rect x="160" y="35" width="130" height="70" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="225" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Low biodiversity:</text>
  <text x="225" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Fragile ecosystem</text>
  <text x="225" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">One species lost →</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Biodiversity = variety of life; high = more stable ecosystem ★</text>
</svg>
options: It makes nature look more colorful and interesting
A diverse ecosystem is more stable and resilient to environmental changes
High biodiversity means more animals to hunt
Biodiversity has no importance for ecosystem stability
correct_answer: A diverse ecosystem is more stable and resilient to environmental changes

template: The Siberian tiger is classified as an endangered species. Which of the following is the MAIN threat to its survival?

  <rect x="15" y="28" width="270" height="84" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="150" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#dc2626">Main threats ★</text>

  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Tiger endangered due to habitat destruction + poaching ★</text>
</svg>

Habitat destruction and poaching by humans

template: Water pollution occurs when harmful substances enter rivers, lakes, or oceans. Which activity is a MAJOR cause of water pollution?

  <text x="80" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#dc2626">Causes ★</text>

  <text x="80" y="79" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">industrial waste ★</text>

template: {CHARACTER_0} learns that Singapore recycles water through NEWater. Why is this important?

  <text x="150" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#0369a1">Why NEWater? ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">Singapore = small island ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">Limited water sources ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">Must recycle water to survive</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">NEWater because Singapore has limited natural water sources ★</text>
</svg>

Because Singapore has limited natural freshwater resources and must manage water sustainably
Because recycled water is cheaper to make than rainwater
Because NEWater is only used for cooling buildings
correct_answer: Because Singapore has limited natural freshwater resources and must manage water sustainably

template: In a food web, a rabbit eats grass, a fox eats rabbits, and an eagle eats both foxes and rabbits. If a disease kills most of the rabbits, what would MOST LIKELY happen to the eagle population?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Food Web — Fox Removed → Eagle Falls</text>
  <defs><marker id="arrFW" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#64748b"/></marker></defs>
  <text x="60" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">🌿 Grass</text>
  <line x1="60" y1="60" x2="60" y2="75" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arrFW)"/>
  <text x="60" y="88" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">🐇 Rabbit</text>
  <line x1="75" y1="88" x2="115" y2="75" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arrFW)"/>
  <text x="150" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">🦊 Fox ✗</text>
  <text x="150" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">(removed)</text>
  <line x1="170" y1="70" x2="205" y2="60" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arrFW)"/>
  <text x="235" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">🦅 Eagle</text>
  <rect x="50" y="104" width="200" height="22" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="150" y="119" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="#dc2626">Fox removed → less food for eagle → eagle ↓ ★</text>
</svg>
options: The eagle population would increase because there is less competition
The eagle population would not be affected
The eagle population would decrease because its food source has reduced
Eagles would stop eating rabbits and only eat foxes
correct_answer: The eagle population would decrease because its food source has reduced

template: In an ecosystem: Seagrass → Dugong → Killer whale. What role do decomposers play in this food chain?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Decomposers — Break Down Dead Organisms</text>
  <rect x="15" y="30" width="270" height="80" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="150" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Role of decomposers ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Break down dead organisms ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Return nutrients to soil ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">e.g. fungi, bacteria</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Decomposers break down dead matter → nutrients back to soil ★</text>
</svg>
options: Decomposers break down dead organisms and return nutrients to the environment for producers to use
Decomposers compete with seagrass for sunlight
Decomposers have no role in this food chain
correct_answer: Decomposers break down dead organisms and return nutrients to the environment for producers to use

template: In a food web: Grass → Grasshopper → Spider → Shrew → Hawk. A farmer uses pesticides that kill all the grasshoppers. What would MOST LIKELY happen to the spider population?

  <text x="195" y="60" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Spider ↓</text>

  <line x1="163" y1="57" x2="193" y2="57" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arrPEST)"/>
  <line x1="215" y1="57" x2="246" y2="57" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arrPEST)"/>

template: Why is the Sun described as the 'source of energy for almost all life on Earth'?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Sun — Energy Source for Life on Earth</text>
  <defs><marker id="arrSUN" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#64748b"/></marker></defs>
  <circle cx="60" cy="70" r="30" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#f97316" stroke-width="2.5"/>
  <text x="60" y="66" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11">☀️</text>
  <text x="60" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#92400e">Sun</text>
  <line x1="90" y1="65" x2="130" y2="55" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arrSUN)"/>
  <text x="155" y="47" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#22c55e">Plants ★</text>
  <text x="155" y="60" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#22c55e">photosynthesis</text>
  <line x1="90" y1="75" x2="130" y2="85" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5" marker-end="url(#arrSUN)"/>
  <text x="160" y="88" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#22c55e">→ food chain ★</text>
  <rect x="40" y="105" width="220" height="18" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Sun → photosynthesis → food chain = energy source ★</text>
</svg>
options: The Sun provides warmth that keeps all animals alive directly
Plants use sunlight for photosynthesis to produce food, which forms the basis of all food chains
The Sun provides light that allows animals to see their food
The Sun evaporates water which provides rain for plants
correct_answer: Plants use sunlight for photosynthesis to produce food, which forms the basis of all food chains

template: Which of these is an example of sustainable living that helps protect the environment?

  <rect x="15" y="30" width="130" height="80" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>

  <text x="80" y="98" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">🌱 Conserve resources</text>

Choosing reusable bags, reducing food waste, and conserving water

template: What does the term 'life cycle' mean?
diagram:

  <text x="150" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Life cycle ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Series of changes an organism</text>
  <text x="150" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">undergoes from birth to death ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">e.g. egg → larva → pupa → adult</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Life cycle = all stages an organism goes through in its lifetime ★</text>
</svg>
options: The list of things a living organism eats
The series of changes an organism goes through from birth to reproduction to death
The habitat where an organism lives
The amount of energy an organism needs
correct_answer: The series of changes an organism goes through from birth to reproduction to death

  <text x="80" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Light energy ★</text>

template: When you push a swing and it moves, what type of energy does the moving swing have?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Moving Swing — Kinetic Energy</text>
  <rect x="15" y="30" width="130" height="80" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="80" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Moving swing ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="62" text-anchor="middle" font-size="16">🏃</text>
  <text x="80" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Kinetic energy ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">(energy of motion)</text>
  <rect x="160" y="35" width="130" height="70" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="225" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">NOT kinetic:</text>
  <text x="225" y="66" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Stationary ball ✗</text>
  <text x="225" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">(potential energy)</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Moving object = KINETIC energy (energy of motion) ★</text>
</svg>

template: When you kick a football, it moves. When does it stop?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Football Stops — Friction + Gravity</text>
  <defs><marker id="arrFOOT" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#64748b"/></marker></defs>
  <text x="50" y="75" text-anchor="middle" font-size="18">⚽</text>
  <line x1="80" y1="72" x2="140" y2="72" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrFOOT)"/>
  <text x="110" y="62" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#ef4444">friction (opp. motion)</text>
  <line x1="150" y1="55" x2="150" y2="85" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrFOOT)"/>
  <text x="175" y="73" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">gravity</text>
  <rect x="20" y="100" width="260" height="22" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="150" y="115" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Football slows: friction (ground) + gravity act on ball ★</text>
</svg>
options: When there is no more air around it
When friction and gravity slow it down and stop it
correct_answer: When friction and gravity slow it down and stop it

template: In the human respiratory system, gas exchange occurs in the alveoli. What gases are exchanged?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Alveoli — Gas Exchange in Lungs</text>
  <defs><marker id="arrALV" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#64748b"/></marker></defs>
  <ellipse cx="150" cy="68" rx="70" ry="42" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5"/>
  <text x="150" y="60" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Alveoli ★</text>
  <line x1="60" y1="55" x2="30" y2="45" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrALV)"/>
  <text x="18" y="40" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#3b82f6">O₂ in ★</text>
  <line x1="60" y1="80" x2="30" y2="90" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrALV)"/>
  <text x="15" y="103" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#ef4444">CO₂ out ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Thin walls + blood</text>
  <text x="150" y="93" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">capillaries ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Alveoli: O₂ in from lungs, CO₂ out into air ★</text>
</svg>
options: Oxygen enters the blood; nitrogen leaves the blood
Oxygen enters the blood from inhaled air; carbon dioxide leaves the blood into the air to be exhaled
Carbon dioxide enters the blood from the air; oxygen leaves the blood
Only oxygen is exchanged in the alveoli
correct_answer: Oxygen enters the blood from inhaled air; carbon dioxide leaves the blood into the air to be exhaled

template: {CHARACTER_0} exercises vigorously. She notices her heart beats faster and she breathes more rapidly. Why does this happen?

  <defs><marker id="arrEX" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#64748b"/></marker></defs>

  <text x="55" y="75" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#92400e">Exercise</text>
  <text x="55" y="89" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#92400e">↑ muscle use</text>
  <line x1="100" y1="67" x2="130" y2="67" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2.5" marker-end="url(#arrEX)"/>

  <text x="211" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Muscles need ★</text>

Her muscles need more oxygen and energy, so the heart and lungs work harder to supply them

template: In a parallel circuit with three identical bulbs, one bulb burns out. What happens to the other two bulbs?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Parallel Circuit — Others Stay Same Brightness</text>
  <rect x="15" y="30" width="130" height="80" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="80" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Parallel ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">One bulb removed ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="83" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Others: SAME</text>
  <text x="80" y="98" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">brightness ★</text>
  <rect x="160" y="35" width="130" height="70" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="225" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Series:</text>
  <text x="225" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">One removed →</text>
  <text x="225" y="84" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">ALL off ✗</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Parallel: one removed, others SAME brightness ★</text>
</svg>
options: Both remaining bulbs also go out
Both remaining bulbs continue to shine with the same brightness
Only one of the remaining bulbs stays on
correct_answer: Both remaining bulbs continue to shine with the same brightness

template: Two batteries are connected in series to a circuit with one bulb. Compared to a circuit with one battery and one bulb, the bulb with two batteries will:
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Two Batteries in Series — Brighter Bulb</text>
  <rect x="15" y="30" width="130" height="80" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="80" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">2 batteries ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">More voltage ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="83" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">More current</text>
  <text x="80" y="98" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">= brighter bulb ★</text>
  <rect x="160" y="35" width="130" height="70" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="225" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">1 battery:</text>
  <text x="225" y="66" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Less voltage ✗</text>
  <text x="225" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Dimmer bulb ✗</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Two batteries in series = more voltage = brighter bulb ★</text>
</svg>
options: Be dimmer because the batteries cancel each other out
Shine brighter because more voltage is provided
Shine at exactly the same brightness
The bulb will immediately burn out
correct_answer: Shine brighter because more voltage is provided

template: A Thermos flask keeps hot drinks hot and cold drinks cold. How does it work?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Thermos Flask — Vacuum Insulates</text>
  <rect x="90" y="25" width="120" height="90" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2" rx="8"/>
  <rect x="102" y="33" width="96" height="78" fill="#0f172a" stroke="#334155" stroke-width="2" rx="6"/>
  <text x="150" y="65" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#94a3b8">VACUUM ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="78" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#94a3b8">(no air/particles)</text>
  <rect x="118" y="43" width="64" height="65" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="2" rx="3"/>
  <text x="150" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#92400e">hot liquid ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="7" fill="#92400e">stays hot ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Vacuum prevents conduction + convection → stays hot ★</text>
</svg>
options: It generates its own heat or cold depending on what is inside
The double-walled vacuum insulation minimizes heat transfer by conduction, convection, and radiation
The metal walls conduct heat to maintain temperature
It uses chemicals inside to heat or cool the liquid
correct_answer: The double-walled vacuum insulation minimizes heat transfer by conduction, convection, and radiation

template: The pitcher plant has a modified leaf shaped like a pitcher filled with liquid. This is an adaptation to help it:
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Pitcher Plant — Traps Insects for Nutrients</text>
  <rect x="15" y="30" width="130" height="80" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="80" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Pitcher plant ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="62" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13">🪲</text>
  <text x="80" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Traps insects ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="94" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Absorbs nitrogen ★</text>
  <rect x="160" y="35" width="130" height="70" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="225" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">Grows in nutrient</text>
  <text x="225" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">poor soil ★</text>
  <text x="225" y="81" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">Bugs = food ★</text>

template: The mangrove tree has prop roots that grow from its trunk and branches into the muddy soil. What is the main function of these prop roots?

  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Mangrove Prop Roots — Structural Support</text>
  <rect x="15" y="30" width="130" height="80" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="80" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Prop roots ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="66" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Support plant in</text>
  <text x="80" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">soft/wet mud ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="94" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Prevent toppling</text>
