  <text x="225" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Make food ✗</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Mangrove prop roots provide structural support in soft mud ★</text>
</svg>
options: To absorb extra sunlight for photosynthesis
To store food for dry seasons
To provide structural support and anchor the tree in soft, waterlogged soil
To filter salt from sea water
correct_answer: To provide structural support and anchor the tree in soft, waterlogged soil

template: Bees and flowers have a mutualistic relationship. What does each organism gain from this interaction?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Bees + Flowers — Mutualism</text>
  <rect x="15" y="30" width="130" height="80" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="80" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Mutualism ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="66" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">🐝 Bee gets nectar ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="81" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">🌺 Flower gets</text>
  <text x="80" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">pollinated ★</text>
  <rect x="160" y="35" width="130" height="70" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="225" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">NOT parasitism:</text>
  <text x="225" y="66" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">BOTH benefit ★</text>
  <text x="225" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">(mutualism) ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Bees + flowers = mutualism (both benefit) ★</text>
</svg>
options: Bees get shelter from the flower; the flower gets water from the bee
Bees get nectar (food) from the flower; the flower gets pollinated, enabling reproduction
Bees protect the flower from insects; the flower provides oxygen to the bee
Both bees and flowers compete for sunlight
correct_answer: Bees get nectar (food) from the flower; the flower gets pollinated, enabling reproduction

template: Tapeworms live inside the human intestine and absorb nutrients from food the human has digested. This type of relationship is called:
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 340 130" width="340" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="170" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Tapeworm — Parasitism</text>
  <rect x="10" y="28" width="155" height="84" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="88" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#dc2626">Parasitism ★</text>
  <text x="88" y="66" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Tapeworm benefits ★</text>
  <text x="88" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Host is harmed ★</text>
  <text x="88" y="94" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">(lives in intestine)</text>
  <rect x="178" y="28" width="155" height="84" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="256" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Mutualism: both +</text>
  <text x="256" y="64" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Commensalism: 1 +, 1 ok</text>
  <text x="256" y="78" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="#dc2626">Parasitism: + & - ★</text>
  <text x="256" y="92" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">(parasite + host -)</text>
  <text x="170" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Tapeworm = parasitism: parasite benefits, host harmed ★</text>
</svg>

template: A flower produces brightly colored petals, sweet nectar, and a strong fragrance. Which of the following is the MOST LIKELY pollination agent for this flower?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">

  <text x="80" y="66" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Bright colors ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Strong scent ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="94" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Nectar produced</text>
  <rect x="160" y="35" width="130" height="70" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="225" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">Wind pollinated:</text>

  <text x="225" y="84" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">Tiny, light pollen</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Bright colored flower = insect pollinated ★</text>
</svg>

template: A seed from a palm tree has a hard, fibrous outer layer that allows it to float. This is an adaptation for which type of seed dispersal?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Palm Seed + Fibrous Coat — Water Dispersal</text>
  <rect x="15" y="30" width="130" height="80" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="80" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Water dispersal ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="64" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">🥥 Fibrous coat ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="78" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Waterproof ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="92" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Floats on water</text>
  <rect x="160" y="35" width="130" height="70" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="225" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">NOT wind:</text>
  <text x="225" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">No wings/light ✗</text>
  <text x="225" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">NOT animal ✗</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Fibrous coat = waterproof + float → water dispersal ★</text>
</svg>

template: A stick placed in a glass of water appears bent at the water surface. This phenomenon is due to:
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Stick in Water Appears Bent — Refraction</text>
  <rect x="15" y="28" width="130" height="84" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="80" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Refraction ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="66" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Light bends at</text>
  <text x="80" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">air–water boundary ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Stick looks bent ★</text>
  <rect x="160" y="35" width="130" height="70" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="225" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">NOT reflection ✗</text>
  <text x="225" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">NOT absorption ✗</text>
  <text x="225" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">It's refraction! ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Stick in water appears bent = light refraction ★</text>
</svg>
options: Reflection of light off the water surface
Refraction — light bends when passing from water to air because it changes speed
The stick is actually bent by the water pressure
Dispersion of white light into colors
correct_answer: Refraction — light bends when passing from water to air because it changes speed

template: Astronauts on the Moon cannot speak to each other through air. They must use radio communication. Why?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">No Sound on Moon — No Air/Medium</text>
  <rect x="15" y="30" width="130" height="80" fill="#0f172a" stroke="#334155" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="80" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="white">Moon 🌙</text>
  <text x="80" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#94a3b8">VACUUM ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="83" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#94a3b8">No air ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="97" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#94a3b8">No medium</text>
  <rect x="160" y="35" width="130" height="70" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="225" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Result ★</text>
  <text x="225" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Sound cannot</text>
  <text x="225" y="84" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">travel ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">No sound on moon = vacuum, no medium for sound to travel ★</text>
</svg>
options: Radio waves travel faster than sound
The Moon has too much gravity for sound to travel
Sound cannot travel in a vacuum (the Moon has no atmosphere/air)
Astronaut helmets absorb all sound waves
correct_answer: Sound cannot travel in a vacuum (the Moon has no atmosphere/air)

template: A guitar player plucks a thick string and a thin string with the same force. Which string produces a lower-pitched sound?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 340 130" width="340" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="170" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Thick String vs Thin — Lower Pitch</text>
  <rect x="10" y="28" width="155" height="84" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="88" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Thick string ★</text>
  <line x1="20" y1="75" x2="155" y2="65" stroke="#334155" stroke-width="6"/>
  <text x="88" y="88" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Vibrates SLOWER ★</text>
  <text x="88" y="100" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Lower pitch ★</text>
  <rect x="178" y="28" width="155" height="84" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="256" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Thin string</text>
  <line x1="188" y1="75" x2="323" y2="65" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="256" y="88" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Vibrates faster ✗</text>
  <text x="256" y="100" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Higher pitch ✗</text>
  <text x="170" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Thick string vibrates slower → lower pitch ★</text>
</svg>
options: Pitch depends on the color of the string

template: Singapore is investing in solar energy. Which statement best explains why solar energy is important for Singapore?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Singapore Solar Energy — Lots of Sunlight</text>
  <rect x="15" y="30" width="270" height="80" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="150" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Why solar is suitable ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">☀️ Singapore near equator</text>
  <text x="150" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Receives lots of sunlight ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">Sunny year-round</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Singapore near equator → lots of sunlight → solar suitable ★</text>
</svg>
options: Singapore receives a lot of sunlight year-round and solar energy reduces dependence on fossil fuels
Solar panels are very cheap to install and maintain
Solar energy can store large amounts of electricity for night use without any technology
Singapore has no other energy options available
correct_answer: Singapore receives a lot of sunlight year-round and solar energy reduces dependence on fossil fuels

template: Singapore's Four National Taps include NEWater, desalination, local catchment water, and imported water. Why does Singapore invest so much in water security?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Singapore 4 Taps — Limited Water</text>
  <rect x="15" y="28" width="270" height="84" fill="#bae6fd" stroke="#0ea5e9" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="150" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#0369a1">4 National Taps ★</text>
  <text x="55" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#0369a1">💧Reservoir</text>
  <text x="150" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#0369a1">🔄 NEWater</text>
  <text x="245" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#0369a1">☔ Rain</text>
  <text x="150" y="90" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#0369a1">🚢 Imported water</text>
  <text x="150" y="103" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">Small island, limited water sources ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">4 taps needed because Singapore has limited water sources ★</text>
</svg>
options: Because Singapore has too much water and needs to manage the excess
Because water is Singapore's only natural resource to export
Because Singapore is a small island nation with limited natural freshwater and vulnerable to climate variability
Because Singapore law requires all water to be recycled
correct_answer: Because Singapore is a small island nation with limited natural freshwater and vulnerable to climate variability

template: {CHARACTER_0} is testing whether plants grow better with music playing nearby. She sets up two groups: one with music (experimental group) and one without music (control group). Why is it important to have a control group?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Control Group — Baseline Comparison</text>
  <rect x="15" y="30" width="270" height="80" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="150" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Control group ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">No treatment applied ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Provides baseline ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">Compare results of treated groups</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Control group = no treatment → provides baseline for comparison ★</text>
</svg>
options: The control group is not necessary in this experiment
The control group provides a baseline for comparison so changes due to music can be identified
The control group shows what happens when all plants die
The control group produces more accurate results than the experimental group
correct_answer: The control group provides a baseline for comparison so changes due to music can be identified

template: Why do cells in different parts of the body look different from each other (e.g., a red blood cell vs. a muscle cell)?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Cells Look Different — Specific Functions</text>
  <rect x="15" y="30" width="270" height="80" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="150" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Why cells differ ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Each cell has a specific</text>
  <text x="150" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">FUNCTION ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">Shape reflects job (e.g. RBC, nerve)</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Cells look different because they're structured for specific functions ★</text>
</svg>
options: Different cells are made of different materials
Cells are shaped and structured differently to suit their specific functions
Cells look different because they are from different organisms
All cells look the same under a microscope
correct_answer: Cells are shaped and structured differently to suit their specific functions
